questions.text: Work is said to be done when:
options: A force acts on an object
questions.explanation: Scientific work requires both a force and a displacement in the direction of the force.

questions.text: The SI unit of work and energy is:
questions.explanation: 1 Joule is the work done by a force of 1N to displace an object by 1m.

questions.text: The energy possessed by an object due to its motion is:
questions.explanation: Kinetic Energy = 1/2 mv².

questions.text: The potential energy of an object of mass 'm' at height 'h' is:
questions.explanation: Gravitational potential energy depends on mass, gravity, and vertical height.

questions.text: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed; it only changes form. This is the:
questions.explanation: Total energy in an isolated system remains constant.

questions.text: The rate of doing work is called:
questions.explanation: Power = Work / Time. SI unit is the Watt (W).

questions.text: One kilowatt-hour (kWh) is equal to:
A coolie carries a heavy load on his head and walks on a horizontal platform. The work done by gravity is:
questions.explanation: Work is zero because the force of gravity and displacement are perpendicular (90 degrees).

questions.text: If velocity of an object is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes:
questions.explanation: KE ∝ v². Doubling velocity makes KE 2² = 4 times.

questions.text: Commercial unit of energy is:
questions.explanation: Electrical energy consumption is measured in kilowatt-hours (units).

questions.text: Work done is zero when the angle between force and displacement is:
questions.explanation: W = Fs cosθ. Since cos 90° = 0, no work is done when force is perpendicular to motion.

questions.text: If a force of 10N displaces a body by 2m in its direction, the work done is:
questions.explanation: W = F * s = 10 * 2 = 20 Joules.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a form of potential energy?
questions.explanation: A running athlete possesses kinetic energy due to motion.

questions.text: Mechanical energy is the sum of:
questions.explanation: Mechanical Energy = Kinetic Energy + Potential Energy.

questions.text: When an object falls from a height, its potential energy ________.
questions.explanation: As height decreases, mgh decreases, and this energy is converted into kinetic energy.

questions.text: One Watt is equal to:
questions.explanation: Power is the rate of work; 1 Watt = 1 Joule per second.

questions.text: If the mass of a moving object is doubled, its kinetic energy:
questions.explanation: KE = ½ mv². KE is directly proportional to mass.

questions.text: An electric bulb of 60W is used for 6 hours daily. Energy consumed in one day is:
questions.explanation: Energy = Power * Time = 0.06 kW * 6 h = 0.36 kWh (units).

questions.text: Work done is negative when:
options: Force is in direction of motion
questions.explanation: Angle is 180°, cos 180° = -1. Example: Work done by friction.

questions.text: The energy stored in a dry cell is ________ energy.
questions.explanation: Cells store energy in the form of chemical bonds.

questions.text: A girl of mass 40kg climbs a height of 5m in 10s. Her power is (g=10):
questions.explanation: Power = Work/Time = mgh/t = (40*10*5)/10 = 200W.

questions.text: Energy is a ________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Energy only has magnitude and no specific direction.

questions.text: The capacity to do work is called:
questions.explanation: An object possessing energy can exert force on another to perform work.

questions.text: Work done by a centripetal force is always ________.
questions.explanation: The force (towards center) is always perpendicular to motion (along tangent).

questions.text: An object is thrown upwards. At the maximum height, it has:
questions.explanation: Velocity is zero (min KE) and height is maximum (max PE).

questions.text: If work is done at a faster rate, ________ is higher.
questions.explanation: Power is defined as the rate of doing work.

questions.text: 1 Joule is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: Work = Force (N) * Displacement (m).

questions.text: In a thermal power plant, heat energy is converted to:
questions.explanation: Steam turns turbines which generate electricity.

questions.text: When a stretched bow releases an arrow, PE converts to:
questions.explanation: The elastic potential energy of the bow moves the arrow.

questions.text: The energy of a body due to its position or configuration is ________.
questions.explanation: This includes gravitational PE (position) and elastic PE (configuration).

questions.text: Can an object have energy without momentum?
questions.explanation: An object at rest has Potential Energy but zero momentum (v=0).

questions.text: Can an object have momentum without energy?
questions.explanation: If it has momentum, it must have velocity, and thus Kinetic Energy.

questions.text: A solar cell converts ________ energy into electrical energy.
questions.explanation: Photovoltaic cells directly convert sunlight to electricity.

questions.text: Total energy of a freely falling body remains ________.
questions.explanation: According to the Law of Conservation of Energy.

questions.text: If we lift a book from the floor, work done by us is ________.
questions.explanation: Force applied and displacement are both upwards.

questions.text: In the previous question, work done by gravity is ________.
questions.explanation: Gravity acts downwards while the book moves upwards.

questions.text: Which unit is used for household electricity bills?
questions.explanation: 1 Unit = 1 kWh.

questions.text: A body at rest can have ________.
questions.explanation: It can have potential energy due to its position.

questions.text: Kinetic energy of a body is ________ to the square of its speed.
questions.explanation: KE = ½ mv².

questions.text: Power is a ________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Power is the ratio of two scalars (Work/Time).
